data.title: Why Is Constellation Brands Stock Falling Today?
data.description:
Constellation Brands, Inc. (NYSE:STZ) shares are down during Friday’s premarket session following the announcement of a CEO succession plan. The stock’s decline comes as the company prepares for a leadership transition, which may be adding pressure as broader markets edged lower.

The Board of Directors has appointed Nicholas Fink as the next President and CEO, effective April 13, succeeding Bill Newlands, who has held the position since 2019. This leadership change is part of a comprehensive succession planning process, with Newlands remaining as a strategic advisor during the transition.

The broader market experienced a decline on the previous trading day, with the Russell 2000 down 0.11% and the S&P 500 falling 0.27%. Constellation’s stock decline aligns with this trend, suggesting that it is caught in the broader sell-off rather than being solely affected by company-specific news.

Technical Analysis

The stock is currently trading 0.67% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 6.60% above its 100-day SMA, indicating some short-term weakness but longer-term strength.

Over the past 12 months, shares have decreased by 0.51% and are positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows, reflecting a relatively stable price range.

The RSI is at 58.30, which is considered neutral territory, while the MACD is below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure on the stock. The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $165.00
  • Key Support: $150.00

Constellation Brands generated 84% of revenue from Mexican beer imports under top-selling brands such as Modelo and Corona. The rest of the business includes some remaining wine and spirits brands, categories where the company has pruned assets in recent years.

With its exclusive rights tied to the Mexican beer brands, effective only in the U.S., the firm has limited revenue exposure to international markets.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Constellation Brands, Inc. is slated to provide its next financial update on April 8, 2026.

  • EPS Estimate: $1.70 (Down from $2.63 YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $1.87 billion (Down from $2.16 billion YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E of 25.5x (Indicates premium valuation)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $168.65. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Barclays: Equal-Weight (Raises Target to $148.00) (Jan. 12)
  • Bernstein: Outperform (Raises Target to $197.00) (Jan. 9)
  • UBS: Buy (Lowers Target to $168.00) (Jan. 9)

Valuation Insight: While the stock trades at a premium P/E multiple, the strong consensus and rising estimates suggest analysts view the growth prospects as justification for the 5% upside to analyst targets.

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Constellation Brands, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: Weak (Score: 28.7) — Trading at a steep premium relative to peers.
  • Quality: Neutral (Score: 48.79) — Balance sheet remains healthy.
  • Momentum: Weak (Score: 25.81) — Stock is underperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: Constellation Brands’ Benzinga Edge signal reveals a mixed outlook. While the Quality score indicates a stable balance sheet, the low Value and Momentum scores suggest that the stock is currently underperforming and trading at a premium, which may warrant caution for potential investors.

Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because STZ carries significant weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

STZ Price Action: Constellation Brands shares were down 1.45% at $160.00 during premarket trading on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: Arista Stock Surges After Q4 Beat, Q1 Sales Outlook Tops Estimates
data.description:
Arista Networks, Inc. (NYSE:ANET) shares are trading higher Friday after the company reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter financial results and issued first-quarter sales guidance above estimates on Thursday after the market closed.

  • Arista Networks shares are climbing with conviction. What’s fueling ANET momentum?

Fourth-Quarter Performance And Outlook

Arista reported adjusted earnings per share of 82 cents, beating the consensus estimate of 76 cents. In addition, the company reported revenue of $2.48 billion, beating the consensus estimate of $2.38 billion.

“Our Q4 results underscore the strong operating leverage inherent in our model, providing a powerful exit rate as we head into 2026,” said Chantelle Breithaupt, Arista’s CFO.

“By pairing 29% revenue growth with a disciplined 47.5% operating margin, Arista achieved a historic milestone: surpassing $1 billion in quarterly net income. Congratulations to the team.”

During the quarter, Arista unveiled its R4 series platforms for AI, data center and routed backbone deployments and expanded Arista AVA with additional agentic AI capabilities for multi-domain event correlation, continuous monitoring and network troubleshooting.

Looking ahead, Arista sees first-quarter revenue of $2.60 billion, versus the consensus estimate of $2.44 billion.

Consensus Ratings And Analyst Action

Arista carries a consensus rating of Buy and holds a consensus price target of $216.26. Following the earnings report, multiple analysts issued price target adjustments:

  • Needham analyst Ryan Koontz maintained a Buy rating on Arista and raised the price target from $165 to $185.
  • Keybanc analyst Brandon Nispel maintained an Overweight rating on Arista and raised the price target from $170 to $178.

Bullish Trend With Conflicting Momentum Signals

Arista Networks is currently trading 6.5% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 10.7% above its 50-day SMA, indicating strong short-term momentum. Shares have increased 23.12% over the past 12 months and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows, suggesting a robust long-term trend.

The RSI is at 48.99, which is considered neutral territory, while MACD is below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure on the stock. The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum, reflecting some uncertainty in the stock’s immediate outlook.

  • Key Support: $125.50
  • Key Resistance: $152.00

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Arista Networks, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: 9.57 — The stock is considered overvalued relative to peers.
  • Growth: 80.56 — Indicates strong growth potential.
  • Quality: 88.02 — Reflects a solid balance sheet and operational efficiency.
  • Momentum: 60.9 — Suggests moderate momentum in the stock’s price action.

The Verdict: Arista’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a company with strong growth and quality metrics, although its value score indicates it may be trading at a premium. Investors should consider the momentum score as a sign of potential price stability, but remain cautious of the high valuation relative to peers.

Arista Shares Climb Higher

ANET Price Action: At the time of publication, Arista shares are trading 7.33% higher at $145.02, according to data from Benzinga Pro.
